'''''Classic Star Wars: Han Solo at Stars' End'' 3''' is the third and final issue of the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] miniseries ''[[Classic Star Wars: Han Solo at Stars' End]]''. It collects material originally printed in the [[1980]]-[[1981]] [[Han Solo at Stars' End (comic strip)|comic strip]] of the same name, which was written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and illustrated by [[Alfredo Alcala]] and adapted of the [[1979]] [[Han Solo at Stars' End|novel of the same name]] by [[Brian Daley]]. The issue was published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[May 14]], [[1997]].

==Publisher's summary==
''To rescue his friend Chewbacca, Han Solo has infiltrated Stars' End, the secret prison where the Corporate Authority keeps its political prisoners. As tough as it was to breach security, however, getting in was the easy part. Now the Rebels have to make their way out!''

==Continuity==
The front matter of ''Classic Star Wars: Han Solo at Stars' End'' 3 dates the events of the issue to [[10 BBY/Legends|10 BBY]]–[[5 BBY/Legends|5 BBY]]. These dates were [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] by the book [[Star Wars: Edge of the Empire Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Edge of the Empire'' Core Rulebook]], which dates the story to [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]].<ref name="EotECR" />
